guidelines: Use semantic <button> element for actions
Ensure 44x44px minimum touch target size
Provide visible focus indicator with 2px outline
Maintain 4.5:1 color contrast ratio for text
Support keyboard activation with Enter and Space
Communicate disabled state with aria-disabled when appropriate

ariaAttributes.usage: Use when button contains only an icon with no visible text
Use for toggle buttons to indicate pressed state
Use when button should be perceived as disabled but remain focusable

focusManagement: Button receives focus on tab. Visible focus ring with 2px ring-offset.
screenReaderGuidance: Announced as 'Button' with its accessible name. If disabled, announced with 'disabled' or 'dimmed'.
commonMistakes: Using div or span instead of button element
Insufficient color contrast in outline variant
Touch target too small for icon buttons
Not disabling pointer events when disabled

guidelines: Always associate input with a visible label using <label> element
Use aria-describedby to associate error messages and hints
Ensure 4.5:1 color contrast for placeholder and input text
Provide clear error messages near the input
Use appropriate input types (email, tel, url) for better mobile keyboards
Mark required fields with aria-required or required attribute

ariaAttributes.usage: Associate with helper text or error messages

focusManagement: Input receives focus ring with 2px offset. Focus moves to input when label is clicked.
screenReaderGuidance: Announced with label, type, required state, and current value. Error messages announced when aria-invalid is true.
commonMistakes: Not associating error messages with aria-describedby
Insufficient color contrast for error states
Not announcing validation errors to screen readers
codeExample:
// Accessible form input with validation

We'll never share your email.

{error && (

{error}

)}

guidelines: Trap focus within dialog while open
Return focus to trigger element on close
Allow closing with Escape key
Provide visible close button
Use role='dialog' and aria-modal='true'
Label dialog with aria-labelledby pointing to title

ariaAttributes.usage: Set to true to indicate modal behavior

keyboardSupport.action: Moves focus to next focusable element within dialog
Moves focus to previous focusable element, cycling within dialog

focusManagement: Focus moves to first focusable element when opened. Focus is trapped within dialog. Focus returns to trigger on close.
screenReaderGuidance: Dialog announced with its title and description. Modal state announced. Close button announced.
commonMistakes: Allowing background content to be interactive
codeExample:
// Accessible dialog implementation
<Dialog open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <DialogTrigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Dialog</Button>
  </DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>Confirm Action</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>
        This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
      </DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setOpen(false)}>
        Cancel
      </Button>
      <Button onClick={handleConfirm}>Confirm</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

guidelines: Associate select with visible label
Support keyboard navigation with arrow keys
Announce selected option to screen readers
Use aria-expanded to indicate open/closed state
Provide clear visual focus indicators
Ensure options are keyboard accessible
focusManagement: Select trigger receives focus. Options are keyboard navigable. Focus returns to trigger after selection.
screenReaderGuidance: Announced as combobox or listbox with label. Current selection announced. Options announced as user navigates.
codeExample:
// Accessible select with error state
{error &&

{error}

}

guidelines: Use role='checkbox' with appropriate aria-checked state
Associate with visible label using htmlFor/id
Ensure 44x44px minimum touch target
Support Space key to toggle
Provide visible focus indicator
Communicate indeterminate state with aria-checked='mixed'

ariaAttributes.usage: Indicates checked state: true, false, or mixed

focusManagement: Checkbox receives focus ring. Focus moves when label is clicked.
screenReaderGuidance: Announced as 'checkbox' with label and checked state. Indeterminate state announced as 'mixed'.
